name: Supporting_Information
title: Supporting Information
description: A selection of codes that provide supporting information about the claim.

concept.definition: The date the weekly NCH database load process cycle begins, during which the claim records are loaded into the Nearline file. This date will always be a Friday, although the claims will actually be appended to the database subsequent to the date.
Number of whole pints of blood furnished to the beneficiary, as reported on the carrier claim (non-DMERC).
A switch indicating whether or not a Managed Care Organization (MCO) has paid the provider for an institutional claim.
This variable is a recoded version of the discharge status code.
The date on a claim for which the covered level of care ended in a general hospital or the active care ended in a psychiatric/tuberculosis hospital.
The beginning date of the beneficiary's non-covered stay.
The ending date of the beneficiary's non-covered stay.
The last date for which the beneficiary has Medicare coverage.
The code indicating whether or not: (1) the claim is from the prospective payment system (PPS), and/or (2) the beneficiary is a deemed insured MQGE (Medicare Qualified Government Employee)
Code system for NCH primary payer code
The beginning date of the beneficiary's qualifying Medicare stay.
The ending date of the beneficiary's qualifying Medicare stay.
The code used to identify those Home Health PPS claims that have 4 visits or less in a 60-day episode.
The code used to identify the means by which the beneficiary was referred for Home Health services.
The type of pharmacy used. This variable indicates the type of pharmacy that dispensed the prescription, as recorded on the PDE.
This variable indicates where the beneficiary lived when the prescription was filled, as reported on the PDE record.

For beneficiaries living in long-term care (LTC) facilities, this variable indicates how many days' supply of the medication was dispensed by the long-term care pharmacy and provides some details about the dispensing event.
This field indicates whether or not the drug is covered by Medicare Part D.
This variable indicates whether the PDE occurred within the catastrophic benefit phase of the Medicare Part D benefit, according to the source PDE.
The HCT/HGB Test Result
Indicates the status of prescription fulfillment.

concept.display: Claim Line Prescription Service Reference Number
concept.definition: Identifies a prescription dispensed by a particular service provider on a particular service date

concept.display: Claim Diagnosis Related Group Outlier Stay Code
concept.definition: On an institutional claim, the code that indicates the beneficiary stay under the prospective payment system (PPS) which, although classified into a specific diagnosis related group, has an unusually long length (day outlier) or exceptionally high cost (cost outlier).
A code defining the type of claim record being processed.
A SET OF RELATED CONDITIONS THAT CO-OCCURRED WITHIN SOME SET OF CLAIMS.
Code indicating the type of claim record being processed with respect to payment (debit/credit indicator; interim/final indicator).
A code indicating the method or format used to submit the claim.
The identification number assigned by CMS to a fiscal intermediary (FI) authorized to process claim records.
Domain of claim adjustment types, including denoting original claims
Code indicating the disposition or outcome of the processing of the claim record.
The code on a non-institutional claim indicating who receives payment or if the claim was denied.
Variable indicates whether or not the provider accepts assignment for the noninstitutional claim.
The reason that no Medicare payment is made for services on an institutional claim.
The type of action requested by the intermediary to be taken on an institutional claim.
A code reported by the provider that indicates the specific type of claim.
A date identifying when data was loaded into the Integrated Data Repository (IDR).
Date a claim or prescription drug event was processed by a CMS front end system, such as the DDPS Operational Data Store.
The number used to identify all items and services provided to a beneficiary during their participation in a clinical trial. (from NCH Claim Clinical Trial Number)
The current status information for the pending and paid claims currently in the system.
A unique tracking number (UTN) assigned to each prior authorization (PA) request.
The contract number of the submitter.
The PBP number of the submitter contract.
The field identifies the claims/claim lines that qualify for specific claims processing edits related to benefit enhancement.
A single character code of 'Y' or 'N' that indicates whether a particular claim/claim line is tied to a PBP benefit enhancement

concept.display: Post Discharge Home Visit Benefit Enhancement Indicator
concept.definition: A single character code of 'Y”'or 'N' that indicates whether a particular claim/claim line is tied to a Post Discharge Home Visit benefit enhancement.
A single character code of “Y” or “N” that indicates whether a particular claim/claim line is tied to a SNF 3-Day Waiver benefit enhancement.
A single character code of “Y” or “N” that indicates whether a particular claim/claime line is tied to a Telehealth benefit enhancement.
A single character code of “Y” or “N” that indicates whether a particular claim/claim line is tied to an AIPBP benefit enhancement.
A single character code of “Y” or “N” that indicates whether a particular claim/claim line is tied to a Care Management Home Visits benefit enhancement.
A status code indicating the paid status of a claim.

concept.display: Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) State Code
concept.definition: Identifies the state where the facility providing services is located.
Identifies the state that the provider providing the service is located in
